text: The surgeon accesses the abdomen at the umbilicus using the Hassan technique, places a mini gel port, and insufflates the abdomen for a single incision laparoscopic appendectomy.
The laparoscopic camera is inserted through the umbilical port, providing a view of the appendix, which shows signs of acute inflammation.

The surgeon grasps the appendix along its mesentery and elevates it, prominently displaying the mesoappendix. Electrocautery is used to divide the mesoappendix, with the hook rocked up and down to ensure hemostasis.
The surgeon clears the proximal appendix of residual fatty mesentery, revealing the base. The appendix is then divided between two PDS endo-loop sutures, ensuring secure ligation.

The surgeon positions an endo-loop over the appendix, using a grasper to pull the appendix through the loop. The loop is cinched down at the base of the appendix. The cecum, appendix, bloodless fold of Treves, and terminal ileum are clearly visible. The first suture is cut, and a second PDS endo-loop is placed distal to the first suture in the same manner.

The surgeon transects the appendix using scissors, leaving a cuff of appendiceal tissue at the specimen's side to prevent suture slippage. The appendix is then extracted through the umbilical port site. The appendiceal base is inspected and appears well occluded and hemostatic.
The laparoscopic camera provides a view of the right fallopian tube and ovary, which appear normal. The pelvic cavity is observed to be free of any fluid.

The surgeon visualizes the left fallopian tube and ovary, confirming their normal appearance. The left and right upper quadrants are inspected, revealing a normal liver, stomach, and gallbladder, with no reactive fluid or pus present in the abdominal region.

The surgeon removes the gel port and reduces the pneumoperitoneum. The fascia is closed with two O-vicryl sutures, followed by infiltration of local anesthetic at the incision site. The skin is closed using monocryl suture, and a cotton ball with a small tegaderm dressing is applied.
